source.title: Psychological and Pharmacologic Treatments for Adults with PTSD

signals: benefits and harms of psychotherapies and pharmacologic agents for treatment
CBT-mixed treatments had high strength of evidence for benefit
Fluoxetine, paroxetine, and venlafaxine had moderate strength of evidence
Family physicians regularly diagnose and treat PTSD
Helping Clinicians Make Better Treatment Choices

extraction.summary: This systematic review evaluates psychological and pharmacologic treatments for adults with PTSD. Cognitive behavior therapy (CBT) and CBT-mixed treatments show high strength of evidence for reducing PTSD symptoms, while fluoxetine, paroxetine, and venlafaxine have moderate evidence for symptom reduction.

population.risk_criteria: Adults exposed to traumatic events (war, natural disaster, violence)
population.disease_definition: Posttraumatic stress disorder (PTSD) diagnosis per Clinician-Administered PTSD Scale

key_thresholds.context: Medium effect size considered clinically significant

key_thresholds.parameter: PTSD prevalence in male combat veterans

recommendations.recommendation: Use cognitive behavior therapy (CBT) and CBT-mixed treatments for PTSD
recommendations.notes: High strength of evidence for improving PTSD symptoms, depression symptoms, and resolution of PTSD diagnosis

recommendations.recommendation: Consider cognitive processing therapy, cognitive therapy, EMDR, and narrative exposure therapy
recommendations.notes: Moderate strength of evidence for benefit

recommendations.recommendation: Screen adults at risk of PTSD with Primary Care PTSD Screen or PTSD Checklist
recommendations.notes: Those exposed to traumatic events should be screened

recommendations.recommendation: Screen adults at risk of PTSD with the Primary Care PTSD Screen or PTSD Checklist
recommendations.notes: For those exposed to traumatic events such as war, natural disaster, or violence

recommendations.recommendation: Conduct structured interview using Clinician-Administered PTSD Scale for those who screen positive
Start with individual trauma-focused psychotherapy as first-line treatment
recommendations.notes: Evidence shows psychotherapy results in greater symptom change with more persistent benefits

recommendations.recommendation: Use pharmacotherapy when psychotherapy is not available
recommendations.notes: Secondary option when first-line therapy unavailable

extraction.follow_up: Use structured interview tools such as Clinician-Administered PTSD Scale for ongoing assessment. Regular monitoring required but specific intervals not defined in document.
extraction.escalation_path: Positive screening with Primary Care PTSD Screen or PTSD Checklist should prompt structured clinical interview using Clinician-Administered PTSD Scale for formal diagnosis and treatment planning.
